data.title: REG - DFS Furniture PLC - Directorate change
data.description:
RNS Number : 6656H DFS Furniture PLC 14 November 2025  

14 November 2025

DFS Furniture PLC

Section 430(2B) Companies Act 2006 Statement - Jo Boydell

The following information is provided in accordance with section 430(2B) of the Companies Act 2006.  Further to the announcement made on 9 April 2025, Jo Boydell stepped down as a Non-Executive Director of DFS Furniture PLC  at the end of the Annual General Meeting on 14 November 2025.

No other remuneration payment or any payment for loss of office of the type specified in Section 430(2B) of the Companies Act 2006 will be made to Jo Boydell after she ceased to be Non-Executive Director of the Company.

In accordance with section 430(2B) of the Companies Act 2006, the information here will remain available on the website until the 2026 Annual Report is made available

Enquiries:

DFS

Liz McDonald (Group Company Secretary)

Phil Hutchinson Investor Relations

investor.relations@dfs.co.uk

About DFS Furniture plc

The Group is the clear market-leading retailer of living room furniture in the United Kingdom. Our Group purpose is to bring great design and comfort into every living room, in an affordable, responsible and sustainable manner. We operate an integrated physical and digital retail network of living room furniture showrooms and web sites in the United Kingdom, Republic of Ireland, trading through our leading brands, DFS and Sofology. We attract customers through our targeted and national marketing activities and our reputation for high quality products and service, breadth of product offer and favourable consumer financing options. We fulfil orders for our exclusive product ranges through our own UK finished goods factories, and through manufacturing partners located in the UK, Europe and Far East, and delivered with care through our expert final-mile delivery service "The Sofa Delivery Company Limited".

data.title: Nexperia committed to continue supply via alternative routes
data.description:
Dutch computer chip maker Nexperia on Friday confirmed it continued to offer alternative supply chain solutions to mitigate the disruption caused by the dispute between the Europe-based unit and its packaging plant in China.

"We want to state clearly that Nexperia has not stopped the shipping of wafers altogether," the Dutch unit said in a statement.

"We are committed to delivering wafers into the supply chain and continue to offer alternative supply chain solutions to mitigate disruption and maintain continuity of delivery as much as possible."

Sources told Reuters this week the company was working with customers on a patch that could relieve some pressure on the auto market, where a scarcity of Nexperia chips has dented some production of cars and parts.

Nexperia on Friday also said that according to its calculations, the Chinese arm should be in the possession of a "sufficient amount of wafers and finished products" to continue operations for several months.

"Therefore, any failure to make shipments is fully the responsibility of Nexperia's entities in China," it added.

A standoff between the European side of the business and its Chinese plant, sparked by the seizure of Nexperia by the Dutch government over fears of technology transfer, has snarled supply of its chips that are key for the global automotive market.

Nexperia makes wafers, the base material for making multiple chips, in Europe, which are then sent to its plant in Dongguan in China where they are cut and packaged. However, due to the standoff the European side has halted shipments to China.

The Chinese government gave some temporary relief to carmakers this week by relaxing export controls on chips made by the Dongguan plant. A Dutch delegation is expected to travel to China next week for further talks.

data.title: Top Analyst Warns Palantir May Be Priced for Perfection Despite Huge Quarter
data.description:
An analyst urged caution on Palantir Technologies even after another strong quarter. Freedom Capital Markets' Almas Almaganbetov raised his price target to $170 from $125 but kept a "Sell" rating, saying the stock may be priced for perfection.

Palantir posted $1.18 billion in revenue in the third quarter and an adjusted operating margin of 51%.

U.S. commercial revenue surged 121% year over year, helping drive record bookings for its AIP platform.

Even so, PLTR shares slid about 7% after the report, trimming gains following a year-to-date rally of more than 127%.

Almaganbetov said growth could soften beyond next year as U.S. commercial momentum normalizes, government and defense budgets tighten and hiring for AI talent pushes costs higher.

He warned current valuation leaves little room for disappointment, creating downside risk if growth slows.

The note highlights the tension between rapid AI adoption and stretched expectations, and may prompt investors to reassess exposure to enterprise AI and defense-focused software names.

data.title: Argentina – TGS lands bond deal
data.description:
By Nic Stone

Transportadora de Gas del Sur has sold a US$500m 10-year non-call five 7.75% senior unsecured note at 98.301 to yield 8%. Price thoughts opened in the low-to-mid 8% area and guidance was set at 8.125%, plus or minus 12.5bp. Books peaked at US$1.3bn and closed at US$1bn, according to a person familiar with the deal.

Barclays, Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, JP Morgan and Balanz were joint bookrunners. Moody's assigned the deal a B2 rating. TGS is the largest gas transport company in Argentina, with more than 9,000km of pipelines. Last month the company was awarded the contract to extend the Perito Moreno trunk gas pipeline from the Vaca Muerta shale play.

data.title: Walmart's returns under McMillon among the strongest of recent departing CEOs
data.description:
By Paolo Laudani

There have been numerous changes among CEOs in the last 18 months, and Walmart's announcement that Chief Executive Doug McMillon will retire in January is only the latest.

Those companies' performances have varied, but Walmart's returns have been among the strongest.

A Reuters analysis of companies that have changed their leadership in the past year-and-a-half, including Nestle and Starbucks , show which ones have performed best under their CEOs' tenure.

In annualized total terms, the company that delivered the best returns to its shareholders is Pandora .

Under the leadership of Alexander Lacik, at the helm since February 2019, the Danish jewelry brand posted annualized total returns of 17%, calculated from the day before the CEO took up office to Nov. 14, 2025.

In September, the firm announced Lacik would retire next March and be succeeded by marketing chief Berta de Pablos-Barbier.

Walmart is second on the list. Its annualized total return came to 15% over the decade since January 2014 with McMillon in charge.

The CEO, who will be replaced by U.S. division chief CEO John Furner, joined in January 2014.

A laggard in this CEO scorecard is German sportswear brand Puma , which under Arne Freundt saw a negative annualized total return of 36.6%. He was replaced in April by former Adidas sales chief Arthur Hoeld.

Looking at total return, French luxury group Kering takes the crown as it delivered a whopping 520% total return to its shareholders in the past 20 years with Francois-Henri Pinault at the company's top position between March 2005 and September of this year.

Walmart comes second in this metric at 419%, while drinks group Diageo , Nike and KitKat maker Nestle were among the worst performers.

data.title: MSC INCOME FUND, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
MSC Income Fund, Inc., a business development company specializing in private loan investments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and strategic shifts in the company's investment strategy, reflecting its commitment to enhancing shareholder value and optimizing its investment portfolio.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Investment Income: $35.4 million, a 6% increase from the corresponding period of 2024, driven by higher dividend and fee income.
  • Net Investment Income: $16.6 million, or $0.35 per share, reflecting a 28% increase from the corresponding period of 2024 due to increased total investment income and decreased expenses.
  • Net Realized Loss: $9.9 million, primarily due to losses from restructures in the Private Loan portfolio.
  • Net Unrealized Appreciation: $21.0 million, resulting from accounting reversals and appreciation in the LMM portfolio.
  • Net Increase in Net Assets Resulting from Operations: $26.5 million, or $0.56 per share, a significant increase from $7.4 million in the corresponding period of 2024, driven by higher net investment income and net unrealized appreciation.

Business Highlights

  • Investment Strategy Shift: MSC Income has shifted its investment strategy to focus solely on its Private Loan investment strategy, moving away from its historical focus on both Private Loan and LMM investment strategies. This change is expected to result in a decrease in the size of the LMM investment portfolio over time as existing investments are repaid or sold.
  • Portfolio Composition: As of September 30, 2025, MSC Income's Private Loan portfolio consisted of 81 companies with a fair value of $750.9 million, while the LMM portfolio included 55 companies with a fair value of $466.7 million. The Private Loan investments are primarily secured by a first priority lien on the assets of the portfolio companies.
  • Investment Portfolio Valuation: The valuation of MSC Income's Investment Portfolio is a critical accounting estimate, with fair value representing 97% of total assets as of September 30, 2025. The valuation process involves significant judgments and subjective measurements.
  • Revenue Recognition: MSC Income records interest and dividend income on an accrual basis and evaluates collectability periodically. Loans or debt securities that become 90 days or more past due are generally placed on non-accrual status.
  • PIK Interest and Cumulative Dividends: MSC Income holds certain debt and preferred equity instruments with PIK interest and cumulative dividend provisions, which are recorded as income but may not be collected until the time of debt principal repayment or equity redemption.
  • Portfolio Asset Quality: As of September 30, 2025, investments on non-accrual status comprised 1.4% of MSC Income's total Investment Portfolio at fair value and 4.6% at cost, indicating a slight improvement from December 31, 2024.
  • Co-Investment Program: MSC Income has received an exemptive order from the SEC permitting co-investments with Main Street and other advisory clients in certain negotiated transactions, allowing for shared investment opportunities.
  • Future Outlook: MSC Income intends to continue executing follow-on investments in existing LMM portfolio companies and expects the size of its Middle Market and Other Portfolio investments to decline as existing investments are repaid or sold.
  • Operational Adjustments: The Adviser has implemented a quarterly cap on the Fund's obligation to reimburse internal administrative services expenses, reflecting a change from historical waivers.
  • Dividend Policy: MSC Income declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share and a supplemental quarterly dividend of $0.01 per share, payable on January 30, 2026, indicating a commitment to returning value to shareholders.

SEC Filing: MSC INCOME FUND, INC. [ MSIF ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: BlackRock's 2 Billion AI Bet: The Data?Center Deal Shaking Up Global Infrastructure
data.description:
BlackRock Inc. just dropped another heavyweight move into the AI-infrastructure race, agreeing to spend up to 2 billion to build a 50-50 data-center venture with Spain's ACS. The structure starts with roughly 1 billion upfront, with the remaining payment tied to milestones, a setup that could be designed to scale around ACS's 1.7 gigawatts of data-center developments. ACS has been leaning deeper into this space after reporting a 138% jump in data-center project orders in 2024, powered by Turner, its US subsidiary that already ranks as the country's largest construction firm.

What's unfolding here is another chapter in the global rush to own the pipes, power, and concrete behind the AI boom. Investors have been piling into anything that touches training computechip makers like Nvidia and SK Hynix, fast-growing startups such as OpenAI and Anthropic, and infrastructure developers feeding hyperscale demand. ACS's portfolio lines up well with that momentum: Turner is already building major data centers for Meta in Indiana and CoreWeave in Pennsylvania, while other subsidiaries are pushing projects across additional regions. The initial assets moving into the new venture span Europe, the US and Australia, with ACS reviewing even more sites across North America, Europe and Asia-Pacific.

This deal also lands just weeks after BlackRock led a consortium on a $40 billion acquisition of Aligned Data Centersone of the sector's largest moves yetpart of a broader push launched in September 2024 with MGX, Microsoft and Nvidia to accelerate AI-infrastructure investment. For ACS chairman Florentino Perez, who is also Real Madrid's president and the firm's largest shareholder, this new partnership could be another step in nudging ACS toward higher-margin digital infrastructure tied to the AI economy.

data.title: RedBird Drops $660 Million Bid for Britain's Telegraph Newspaper — WSJ
data.description:
By Ben Dummett

RedBird Capital Partners has abandoned its $660 million deal for Britain's Daily Telegraph, further drawing out a yearslong saga for control of the 170-year-old newspaper.

The U.S. buyout firm, headed by former Goldman Sachs banker Gerry Cardinale, dropped its bid Friday, saying it would work to help secure an alternative solution for the publication.

RedBird dropped its bid because of concerns about securing regulatory approval and backlash against the deal from Telegraph staff, according to a person familiar with the matter. The newspaper has recently run several articles criticizing RedBird Chairman John Thornton's ties to China.

The collapse of the deal is the latest twist in a protected tussle for control of The Telegraph, a politically influential paper with strong ties to Britain's Conservative Party.

The publication was effectively put up for sale in 2023 after its longtime owner ran into financial difficulties.

That year, RedBird partnered with IMI — an U.A.E. investment group — to effectively buy the Telegraph, together with its smaller sister publication the Spectator.

However, that deal was then blocked by the U.K. government amid concerns about the prospect of indirect foreign government involvement in a British publication. That forced the RedBird IMI venture to sell the business.

RedBird IMI was able to sell the Spectator to British hedge-fund billionaire Paul Marshall last year. However, an attempt to sell the Telegraph to a group of investors led by Dovid Efune, the owner and publisher of the New York Sun, fell flat.

In May, RedBird stepped in to acquire control of the newspaper in a deal that valued the business at GBP500 million, including debt — roughly $660 million.

Under the deal, IMI's ownership was to be capped at 15% to meet Britain's proposed rules governing foreign-state ownership of domestic media outlets.

RedBird had also aimed to bring on-board U.K.-based investors to participate in the deal as minority shareholders.

Write to Ben Dummett at ben.dummett@wsj.com

data.title: Markets slide, investors worry about Fed and megacap tech
data.description:
Wall Street's main indexes sank on Friday, weighed down by technology stocks, while Federal Reserve officials cast doubt on a potential interest rate cut in December.

Concerns about stretched AI stock valuations have triggered declines in recent weeks, putting the Nasdaq on course for its longest losing streak since April.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell about 0.6% in morning trading in New York. The FTSE 100 slid more than 1%.

Expectations for a 25-point rate cut in December fell to 53% from last week's 67%, according to CME Group's FedWatch tool, after policy makers expressed reticence on more monetary easing.

QUOTES:

SEEMA SHAH, CHIEF GLOBAL STRATEGIST, PRINCIPAL GLOBAL INVESTORS, LONDON:

"Markets are getting a view that the labour market is slowing down. But because the Fed doesn't have enough confidence in the alternate data, they would rather still hold.

"So you have markets that are concerned about the growth outlook. If the data is correct in the way it seems to be trending, the economy does require rate cuts.

"This is where, for the first time, the government shutdown really does have a sustained impact on the economy.

"So, we just have to see if that data is going to come through. It always makes sense for the Fed to step in ahead of time rather than waiting for the (unemployment) numbers to start moving higher.

"The lack of a Fed cut in December would be quite negative. And we would expect that the market would continue to respond quite negatively until we get an indication that a Fed rate cut is coming.

"We are overall overweight in equities and we're still overweight U.S. equities."

BILL FITZPATRICK, MANAGING DIRECTOR AND PORTFOLIO MANAGER AT LOGAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, CHICAGO:

"There's a bit of a natural unwind in that valuations were a bit lofty in some of the big tech names, and you had a few large investors lightening up on their positions. I think it just speaks to the point that the starting point does matter and these are terrific companies, their stock price has performed very well and to get a bit of a rotation is not too surprising.

"The hope is that instead of a bubble mentality, we just get a broadening-out of some of the leadership. Some of the more stable sectors that have not participated the last couple of years are starting to gain more recognition and perhaps we'll see some more stable sectors do well.

"Investors should be continuing to diversify their portfolio away from the one sector that has been driving the bus for so long. The message is to continue to diversify broadening-out the leadership. The timing of this sell-off or unwind is going to be too difficult to gauge."

MATTHEW PALLAI, CHIEF INVESTMENT OFFICER AT NOMURA CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, NEW YORK:

"I don’t think this is a bubble, so to speak, either in credit or on the equity side. However, I do think there is a significant amount of liquidity that has been out there in the market and that is driving both.

"It has driven credit spreads to fairly tight levels in several but not all credit markets, and some widening is probably healthy for the market at the moment, given the fact that in the macro backdrop there’s more uncertainty now around things like unemployment and tariffs and geopolitics.

"On the equity side... markets have done well for a fairly long period of time now, and people are willing to buy the dip, and if that continues to happen over and over again we can get to levels that are pretty steamy. I think it’s healthy to have some correction from those levels, especially if it’s on the backdrop of more uncertainty in the macro environment."

data.title: Up Digital Revamps Edmonton SEO Service with New AI-Driven Optimization Framework
data.description:
Edmonton, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) -  Up Digital announced today that it has updated its Edmonton SEO service to focus on AI-driven optimization. The new service is designed to help local businesses appear more consistently in AI assistants, generative search platforms, and traditional search engines by improving how their websites are understood and indexed by machine-learning systems.

What is Up Digital's new AI SEO service?

Up Digital's AI SEO service is a redesigned optimization program that uses machine learning to audit websites, structure content, and improve online visibility. It focuses on how search engines and AI systems interpret topics, entities, and brand information. The goal is to help websites become easier for AI models to reference and recommend.

How does Up Digital improve AI visibility for businesses?

The service analyzes how often a business is recognized, cited, or mentioned by AI platforms such as ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews. It identifies missing entities, unclear page structures, and weak topical authority. Up Digital then restructures content and metadata so AI systems can more accurately identify the business and use it in their generated answers.

Why is AI SEO becoming important in Edmonton?

People are now using AI assistants to find services, ask questions, and compare local providers. These systems rely less on traditional keyword rankings and more on structured data, entity relevance, and semantic clarity. Up Digital introduced AI SEO to help Edmonton organizations adapt to this shift and maintain visibility across multiple discovery platforms.

How is AI SEO different from traditional SEO?

Traditional SEO focuses on improving rankings in search engine results pages. AI SEO focuses on improving how AI models interpret, categorize, and reference a business when answering questions. This includes entity mapping, citation patterns, and content structures that align with machine-learning inputs rather than manual ranking signals alone.

Does Up Digital offer AI SEO specifically for local businesses in Edmonton?

Yes. The updated service is built for Edmonton organizations that want stronger visibility in both organic search results and conversational AI platforms. It includes local entity optimization, location-based schema, and audits that measure how well a business appears in AI-generated summaries.

How does Up Digital measure improvements in AI visibility?

Up Digital tracks entity recognition, topic authority scores, structured data accuracy, and how frequently a business appears in AI-generated responses. These metrics create a visibility baseline that can be monitored monthly. Clients receive reports that show improvements across traditional search engines and modern AI platforms.

About Up Digital

Up Digital is a digital marketing agency based in Edmonton, Alberta. The company provides SEO, paid advertising, and online growth services for businesses across Canada. Up Digital focuses on measurable performance, transparent reporting, and modern optimization methods designed for both search engines and AI systems.

Contact:

Up Digital

800-581-1766

Email: info@updigital.ca

Website: www.updigital.ca

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/274520

data.title: AI and Quantum Players See Steep Pullback as Market Demands Revenue Clarity
data.description:
A group of high-growth tech names tied to AI, quantum computing and new-energy sectors dropped about 30% from mid-October peaks over the past month.

Investors shifted focus when third-quarter earnings season highlighted companies with clear profits and cash flow. FactSet data showed roughly 82% of S&P 500 firms beat earnings estimates and about 77% topped revenue forecasts, prompting money to move into businesses with steadier fundamentals.

That rotation helped the iShares MSCI USA Value Factor ETF rise roughly 6% as capital flowed toward value and profit-oriented names. Traders grew more cautious about early-stage stories with thin revenue and narrow margins.

Quantum computing firms saw extra pressure after hopes for federal backing faded. Reports that U.S. agencies might take stakes in key quantum players failed to materialize, and investors pulled back from speculative bets.

AI-linked companies also faced scrutiny. CoreWeave and SoundHound AI had strong runs earlier this year, but investors now want clearer signs that high growth will translate into consistent revenue and margins.

The second challenge that the group would face is the next earnings call and policy changes. Analysts explain that they will scrutinize the fundamentals, quality of revenue and the trend of the margins and cash flows to identify which names would recover and which would require longer.

data.title: Scholar Rock Shares Rise on Renewed Apitegromab Hopes
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Scholar Rock Holding shares rose sharply Friday after the biopharmaceutical company gave a positive update on its efforts to win Food and Drug Administration approval of its proposed apitegromab treatment for the rare genetic neuromuscular disease spinal muscular atrophy.

Shares of the Cambridge, Mass., company were recently changing hands at $38.29, up 27%.

Scholar Rock said it had a constructive and collaborative meeting this week with the FDA and contract manufacturer Catalent Indiana regarding its application for apitegromab, which the FDA rejected in September due to observations identified during a routine general site inspection of the Catalent fill-finish plant, which was acquired by Novo Nordisk last year.

Scholar Rock said Catalent presented progress it has made in implementing a remediation plan and confirmed that the site is on track to be ready for FDA reinspection by the end of the year.

The company said it anticipates a resubmission of its apitegromab application and a potential U.S. launch of the drug next year, pending FDA approval.

Scholar Rock has previously said an FDA green light would make apitegromab a first-in-class muscle-targeted therapy with the potential to establish a new standard of care in the treatment of spinal muscular atrophy, which is characterized by the irreversible loss of motor neurons, atrophy of the voluntary muscles of the limbs and trunk, and progressive muscle wasting that causes continuous motor function decline.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: MetroCity Bankshares, Inc. Announces Expected Merger Completion
data.description:

  • Regulatory approvals and shareholder consent already obtained.
  • Merger completion subject to final closing conditions.
  • MetroCity to be the surviving entity post-merger.

data.title: Fitch Ratings: US CRE Refinancing Stability Underpinned by Resilient Debt Capital Markets
data.description:
Related Content: U.S. Commercial Real Estate CMBS Loan Performance Monitor: 2H25 - Steady Refinancing Conditions Underpinned by Resilient CRE Debt Capital Markets

Fitch Ratings-Chicago/Toronto-14 November 2025: U.S. commercial real estate (CRE) loan refinancing remains resilient, with ample liquidity absorbing this year’s elevated maturities, says Fitch Ratings. However, the ongoing office sector correction continues to push CMBS delinquencies higher, according to Fitch Ratings’ U.S. Commercial Real Estate CMBS Loan Performance Monitor: 2H25.

Fitch expects rising delinquencies to persist into 2026 amid a slowing economy, uncertainty around tariff and policy impact, and a cooling labor market. However, the pace should moderate, supported by steady new issuance, active modifications, and further anticipated rate cuts.

Repayments in 2025 tracked 2024 levels as strong liquidity and a more stable interest rate environment supported refinancing conditions. Through 3Q25, Fitch tracked $28.8 billion (1,723 loans) of non-defaulted, non-defeased U.S. CMBS conduit and Freddie Mac loans maturing in 2025. Of these, borrowers repaid $19.9 billion (1,444 loans), for a 69% refinance rate by balance (84% by count), comparable to 68% and 84%, respectively, in 2024. Retail and hotel repayments slowed, while office gained momentum.

For 2026, Fitch projects overall refinancing to remain broadly in line with 2025, with 69% by balance (78% by count) meeting our refinance stresses. We also expect office and retail activity to improve, with higher urban office and regional mall repayment rates following multiple weak quarters.

Fitch’s projections incorporate refinance stress testing on 2026-2027 maturing conduit and Freddie Mac loans, assessing performance against debt service coverage ratio (DSCR) and loan-to-value (LTV) thresholds at current market capitalization rates, interest rates, and insight from recent CMBS transactions. Fitch has updated its methodology for projecting refinanceability to incorporate a simplified composite scoring approach, which, based on observed refinance outcomes, is more predictive and allows the relative strength of loan performance to influence refinance forecasts.

The full report is available at www.fitchratings.com or by clicking the link above.
